title: 15-minute DNA extraction from beetle legs for PCR barcoding
author: Chase Mayers

steps_raw.instruction: Use a pipette to transfer 50 µL of PrepMan Ultra Sample Preparation Reagent into a small microcentrifuge tube. Label the tube appropriately. If possible, this should be done in a sterile environment and away from PCR equiptment to avoid contaminating the PrepMan reagent with unwanted DNA.

Using sterile forceps, remove one leg (or multiple) from the beetle and transfer to the PrepMan reagant. Fresh, dried, and ethanol-pickled beetle specimens have all yielded usable results with varying success rates.
Using a sterile transfer needle (or other tool), thoroughly crush the leg(s) against the inner wall of the microcentrifuge tube.
Incubate the microcentrifuge tube at 100 °C for 00:10:00 .
Optional: Centrifuge the microcentrifuge tube briefly and pipette the supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube. Label appropriately.
The liquid in the tube is now ready to be used as template for PCR. It can be stored at -20 °C . Typically we used
